summary: Implementation-stage handoff for the currently materialized explicit testcase sec-e2e-024. The handoff freezes src/qwenpaw/security as the owning audit-foundation boundary, freezes the read-only entrypoint under tests/integration/security, constrains that entrypoint to the real app subprocess and isolated working directory, and hardens it against soft happy-path bypasses by requiring tool-boundary context spying, physical audit-ledger inspection, hash-chain verification, and pre-execution evidence ordering. The app subprocess now starts successfully (startup_error=None, segno and all previously missing deps have been installed); the entrypoint fails with pure business-implementation Non_Repudiation_Gap signals that define the Coding/Repair repair queue.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Authenticate an employee, request a delegated high-risk action through the real app subprocess and agent/plugin seam, then provide the required confirmation through the security harness.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: The resulting observations must prove tool-boundary implicit context propagation, a continuous employee -> agent -> plugin -> tool evidence chain, a physical USER_CONFIRMATION record on disk inside the isolated working directory with the expected digest, local hash-chain integrity, and the order 'store evidence before releasing the high-risk tool'.
explicitEntrypoints.failureReason: AssertionError category="Non_Repudiation_Gap": the app subprocess starts successfully but the audit foundation is not yet implemented. The following business capabilities are all missing: tool-boundary implicit user-identity context spy, durable USER_CONFIRMATION record on disk with payload digest and prior-hash anchor, local hash-chain integrity verifier, continuity anchor artifact, human approval gate, and the pre-execution evidence ordering guarantee (store evidence before releasing the high-risk tool). Coding/Repair must realize all of these inside src/qwenpaw/security.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: root-architecture-contracts
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Execute the root architecture contract guard from the repository root.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard confirms that the root contract references the required local contracts, including the new security contracts.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: root-architecture-deliverables
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Execute the root architecture deliverables guard from the repository root.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard confirms that the expected architecture files and failure-record artifact exist at stable paths.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: validator-bootstrap-traceability
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Execute the repository-native Node guardrail script directly from the workspace root.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The script confirms that package.json validation commands point to the bundled validator scripts and that the validator contract names the same assets.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-audit-contract-boundaries
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Execute the security boundary guard from the repository root.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard confirms that OVERALL_ARCHITECTURE.md, src/qwenpaw/ARCHITECTURE.md, src/qwenpaw/security/ARCHITECTURE.md, tests/ARCHITECTURE.md, and tests/integration/security/ARCHITECTURE.md all point to the same sec-e2e-024 owning boundary.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-explicit-entrypoint-traceability
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Execute the sec-e2e-024 traceability guard from the repository root.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard confirms that SystemArchitecture.json, ImplementationToCodingHandoff.json, and test-failure-records.json all point to the same read-only sec-e2e-024 entrypoint, that the entrypoint still contains the frozen runtime-inspection methods, and that the shared real-environment bootstrap keeps the readable startup-failure surface.

supportingNonExplicitTests.name: security-config-suite
supportingNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Run the existing integration security configuration module.
supportingNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: Security configuration endpoints continue to round-trip global and agent-scoped file-guard, tool-guard, and skill-scanner settings.

codingTargets.failureSignal: If sec-e2e-024 fails with Non_Repudiation_Gap, either the shared real-environment bootstrap is still incomplete for the live app subprocess, or the runtime still lacks a tool-boundary user-identity context spy, a tamper-evident physical confirmation record with digest and prior-hash, a local hash-chain verifier plus continuity anchor, and a hard guarantee that evidence is stored before the high-risk tool effect.
codingTargets.nextAction: Realize the audit foundation inside src/qwenpaw/security: (1) add a tool-boundary user-identity context spy that reads from contextvars without explicit parameter threading; (2) implement a durable hash-linked confirmation record writer that stores a USER_CONFIRMATION artifact with payload_digest and prior_hash before releasing the high-risk tool; (3) implement a local hash-chain verifier; (4) implement a continuity anchor emitter; (5) implement a human approval gate that emits a pending approval record; (6) implement a Security Center query seam that projects the employee -> agent -> plugin -> tool lineage as a queryable runtime trace. Adapt app/agent_context.py, app/approvals/service.py, app/inbox_trace_store.py, and the high-risk execution gate behind that contract. Keep tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py unchanged.

openGaps: The current sec-e2e-024 explicit entrypoint is intentionally failing with business-implementation Non_Repudiation_Gap signals. The app subprocess starts successfully (all declared bootstrap dependencies including segno are now installed). Coding/Repair must implement the audit foundation inside src/qwenpaw/security to make this test pass.

The remaining explicit security scenarios from design/KG/SystemArchitecture.json (sec-e2e-025, sec-e2e-026, sec-e2e-027, sec-e2e-021, sec-e2e-022, sec-e2e-023) are still unmaterialized in this focused sec-e2e-024 design pass.
